rag_rules: Place implementation-level content only in implementation scope; keep type-level content out of implementation-specific ownership decisions.
Preserve existing architecture by attaching new content to the correct owning declaration without changing subcomponents, features, or connections.
Respect scope boundaries: names valid only through subcomponents or connections belong in implementation-owned content, not type-owned content.
generic_rules: Maintain owner/scope consistency between declarations and the identifiers they reference.
Insert new annex content after the relevant structural elements in the same owner so referenced elements are already declared.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Keep the original AADL structure intact and add only the intended annex block to the chosen owner.
Implementation-owned bindings may reference implementation-visible paths; type-owned content should use only type-visible interface names.
anti_patterns: Do not move, rewrite, or delete existing architectural declarations when adding annex content.
Do not place content under an owner where its referenced identifiers are out of scope.
Do not use implementation-only paths in content owned by a component type.
irrelevant_topics: Detailed AGREE clause syntax forms and expression typing rules are not needed for owner/scope fusion decisions.
Boolean-expression contract examples are not needed for structural insertion decisions.
Property-as-variable validator repair guidance is not relevant unless diagnostics are active.
